teachingPoint: T621 SagaOrchestrator MUST persist saga state before dispatching each step (persist-before-dispatch). storeDocumentWithOCC(versionPin:-1) at ORDER 1 ensures the saga record exists before any step fires. SETNX step lock (step-lock:{sagaId}:{stepIndex}) acquired BEFORE executing the step body — prevents concurrent duplicate step execution on crash replay. Compensation strategy registered at ORDER 2 before step body runs. storeDocument(saga) MUST precede enqueue(SagaStepExecuted) at every transition (DNA-8).
positiveExample: T621: storeDocumentWithOCC(sagaState, {sagaId, status:RUNNING, versionPin:-1}) at ORDER 1; SETNX(step-lock:{sagaId}:{stepIndex}) at ORDER 2; registerCompensation(stepIndex, strategy) at ORDER 3; executeStepBody() at ORDER 4; storeDocument(checkpoint) BEFORE enqueue(SagaStepExecuted) at ORDER 5.
negativeExample: T621 calls enqueue(SagaStepExecuted) before storeDocument(saga) — on crash before the store, the step appears complete but no checkpoint exists. Replay skips the step, leaving a gap. Or T621 acquires SETNX after step body runs — two concurrent instances both execute the step body, causing duplicate side effects.
discriminatingConstraint: storeDocumentWithOCC versionPin:-1 at ORDER 1; SETNX step-lock at ORDER 2 before step body; storeDocument BEFORE enqueue at every transition. Any reorder = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: SagaOrchestrator storeDocumentWithOCC versionPin -1 SETNX step-lock persist-before-dispatch compensation T621 DNA-8 CF-19-1 checkpoint

teachingPoint: T622 CompensationEngine MUST execute compensations in strict serial reverse order (LIFO). stop-on-first-failure means if compensation step N fails, the engine stops and emits CompensationFailed without attempting steps N-1..0. idempotent restart: each compensation step checks SETNX(comp-lock:{sagaId}:{stepIndex}) before executing — prevents duplicate compensation execution on crash replay. compensationStack is immutable after registration — no modifications during execution.
positiveExample: T622: iterate compensationStack in reverse (LIFO); for each step SETNX(comp-lock:{sagaId}:{step}) at ORDER 1; execute compensation body; storeDocument(compensationRecord) BEFORE enqueue(CompensationStepExecuted) — DNA-8; on step failure emit CompensationFailed with failedStep and halt — do not continue to next step.
negativeExample: T622 executes compensations in parallel (Promise.all) — parallel compensations create race conditions where step-N side effects are partially undone. Or T622 continues after first failure (best-effort) — partially compensated saga is now in an undefined state. Or T622 skips SETNX on compensation — duplicate compensation re-runs on crash, double-refund risk.
discriminatingConstraint: Serial LIFO order; stop-on-first-failure; SETNX per compensation step; storeDocument BEFORE emit per step. Parallel or best-effort execution = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: CompensationEngine LIFO serial stop-on-first-failure idempotent restart SETNX comp-lock T622 CF-19-2 compensationStack reverse

teachingPoint: T623 ComplianceAuditWriter MUST write immutable compliance records with retentionExpiresAt computed at write time (never deferred). Records are stored in a PLATFORM_ONLY append-only index — no updateDocument, no deleteDocument on compliance records ever. retentionExpiresAt = writtenAt + retentionDays (from FREEDOM config, never hardcoded). ComplianceRecordWritten MUST be emitted AFTER storeDocument — DNA-8. auditHash (SHA-256 of tenantId + sagaId + eventType + writtenAt) included for tamper detection.
positiveExample: T623: compute retentionExpiresAt = writtenAt + freedom.get('compliance_retention_days') days at ORDER 1; compute auditHash = sha256(tenantId+sagaId+eventType+writtenAt) at ORDER 2; storeDocument(xiigen-compliance-records, record, auditId) — append-only, PLATFORM_ONLY at ORDER 3; enqueue(ComplianceRecordWritten) at ORDER 4. No updateDocument ever.
negativeExample: T623 sets retentionExpiresAt = null and computes it later in a batch job — retention window is undefined during the record's early life. Or T623 calls updateDocument to amend a compliance record (e.g., to add an audit result) — immutability broken. Or T623 hardcodes retentionDays = 365 instead of reading from FREEDOM config.
discriminatingConstraint: retentionExpiresAt computed at write time from FREEDOM config; append-only store (no update/delete); storeDocument BEFORE emit. Any deferred retention or mutability = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: ComplianceAuditWriter retentionExpiresAt write time FREEDOM config append-only PLATFORM_ONLY auditHash SHA-256 T623 CF-19-3 immutable

teachingPoint: T624 DataRetentionEnforcer MUST gate purge behind dual approval: retentionExpiresAt < now AND legalHoldActive === false (both gates required). archive-before-delete: storeDocument(archive index) MUST complete before deleteDocument on any record. dynamic CRON schedule from FREEDOM config (retention_cron_schedule) — never hardcoded. PurgeCompletedEvent includes archiveRef for audit trail. On legalHold = true, emit RetentionHoldActive and skip purge entirely.
positiveExample: T624: load retention config from FREEDOM; for each expired record: check legalHoldActive — if true emit RetentionHoldActive and skip; storeDocument(xiigen-retention-archive, archivedRecord) at ORDER 1; deleteDocument(original) at ORDER 2 — only after archive confirmed; emit PurgeCompleted with archiveRef. CRON schedule from freedom.get('retention_cron_schedule').
negativeExample: T624 calls deleteDocument first and archives 'if time permits' — data permanently lost if archive fails after delete. Or T624 purges even when legalHoldActive = true — legal hold violation. Or T624 hardcodes '@daily' as the cron schedule — FREEDOM config bypass. Or T624 skips the archive step entirely for 'expired records older than 7 years'.
discriminatingConstraint: dual gate: retentionExpired AND NOT legalHold; archive-before-delete; CRON from FREEDOM config. Any delete-before-archive or legalHold bypass = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: DataRetentionEnforcer dual gate retentionExpiresAt legalHold archive before delete CRON FREEDOM config PurgeCompleted archiveRef T624 CF-19-4

teachingPoint: OCC (Optimistic Concurrency Control) with versionPin:-1 for new documents vs versionPin:N for updates is the correct pattern for saga state writes. versionPin:-1 signals 'document must not exist yet' — equivalent to INSERT IF NOT EXISTS. On conflict (document already exists), the OCC layer returns OCC_CONFLICT rather than silently overwriting. This prevents duplicate saga initialization on crash replay, where the bootstrapper restarts and tries to re-initialize a saga already in RUNNING state.
positiveExample: storeDocumentWithOCC(xiigen-sagas, {sagaId, status:'RUNNING', ...}, sagaId, {versionPin: -1}) — if sagaId already exists (crash replay), OCC returns conflict; handler emits SagaAlreadyRunning and returns success (idempotent). For step updates: storeDocumentWithOCC(sagaId, {status:'STEP_COMPLETE', stepIndex}, sagaId, {versionPin: currentVersion}).
negativeExample: plain storeDocument(xiigen-sagas, {sagaId, status:'RUNNING'}) — crash replay overwrites the existing RUNNING state, resetting stepIndex to 0 and losing all completed steps. Or storeDocumentWithOCC with versionPin:0 instead of -1 — version 0 exists after first write, so versionPin:0 will conflict on the second write, not the first.
discriminatingConstraint: versionPin:-1 for initial insert; versionPin:currentVersion for updates; OCC_CONFLICT → idempotent return. Plain storeDocument for saga init = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: OCC versionPin -1 new document insert saga state storeDocumentWithOCC crash replay SagaAlreadyRunning idempotent T621 CF-19-1

teachingPoint: Compliance records require a cryptographic audit hash for tamper detection. auditHash = SHA-256(tenantId + sagaId + eventType + writtenAt) provides a deterministic fingerprint per record. The hash must be computed from fields that uniquely identify the event — including writtenAt timestamp — so two records for the same event at different times produce different hashes. Audit verification re-computes the hash and compares — any mutation of the stored record is detectable. knowledgeScope PLATFORM_ONLY means the compliance index is inaccessible to tenant-scoped fabric queries.
positiveExample: const auditId = createHash('sha256').update([tenantId, sagaId, eventType, writtenAt].join(':')).digest('hex'); storeDocument('xiigen-compliance-records', {auditId, tenantId, sagaId, eventType, writtenAt, retentionExpiresAt, knowledgeScope:'PLATFORM_ONLY'}, auditId);
negativeExample: auditHash = uuid() — random UUID is not deterministic; re-computation for verification will always differ. Or auditHash = md5(sagaId) — weak hash and excludes eventType and writtenAt, making two audit records for different events on the same saga produce the same hash.
discriminatingConstraint: SHA-256 of [tenantId, sagaId, eventType, writtenAt] joined — any weaker hash or non-deterministic ID = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: auditHash SHA-256 tamper detection compliance record T623 tenantId sagaId eventType writtenAt deterministic PLATFORM_ONLY

teachingPoint: SAGA-PERSIST-BEFORE-DISPATCH-001: Saga state must be persisted before any step is dispatched to the queue. This is an extension of the DNA-8 outbox pattern applied to multi-step orchestration. If the saga record does not exist when a step fires, crash recovery cannot determine which steps completed. storeDocumentWithOCC at ORDER 1 creates the saga record; the step is dispatched only after the store confirms success. The OCC versionPin:-1 guard prevents double-initialization on replay.
positiveExample: storeDocumentWithOCC(sagaId, {status:RUNNING, versionPin:-1}) ORDER 1 → acquire SETNX ORDER 2 → execute step → storeDocument(checkpoint) → enqueue(SagaStepExecuted). All DNA-8.
negativeExample: enqueue(SagaStepExecuted) before storeDocumentWithOCC — crash before store leaves orphaned queue message with no saga state.
discriminatingConstraint: SAGA-PERSIST-BEFORE-DISPATCH-001: store ORDER 1; enqueue always after store.
keywords: SAGA-PERSIST-BEFORE-DISPATCH-001 saga persist before dispatch DNA-8 outbox storeDocumentWithOCC versionPin -1

teachingPoint: COMPLIANCE-APPEND-ONLY-001: Compliance audit records are immutable by design. storeDocument (append) is the only permitted write operation on compliance indices. updateDocument and deleteDocument are BUILD_FAILURE violations on compliance indices. knowledgeScope PLATFORM_ONLY prevents tenant-scoped read access. retentionExpiresAt computed at write time from FREEDOM config ensures legal hold periods are captured even if policy changes later.
positiveExample: storeDocument('xiigen-compliance-records', {auditId, tenantId, sagaId, writtenAt, retentionExpiresAt, knowledgeScope:'PLATFORM_ONLY'}, auditId) — new record per audit event, no updates.
negativeExample: updateDocument('xiigen-compliance-records', auditId, {status:'VERIFIED'}) — mutates the immutable compliance record, breaking the audit trail.
discriminatingConstraint: COMPLIANCE-APPEND-ONLY-001: only storeDocument on compliance index; retentionExpiresAt at write time. updateDocument/deleteDocument = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: COMPLIANCE-APPEND-ONLY-001 compliance append only immutable platform only retentionExpiresAt FREEDOM config

teachingPoint: RETENTION-DUAL-GATE-001: Data retention purge requires two independent gates before any delete: (1) retentionExpiresAt < now confirms the record is past its retention window; (2) legalHoldActive === false confirms no legal hold is in place. Both gates must pass independently — legal hold supersedes expiry. archive-before-delete is mandatory: archive record must be stored successfully before the original is deleted. CRON schedule from FREEDOM config — never hardcoded.
positiveExample: if (record.retentionExpiresAt < now && !record.legalHoldActive) { await storeDocument(archiveIndex, record); await deleteDocument(sourceIndex, id); }
negativeExample: if (record.retentionExpiresAt < now) { await deleteDocument(sourceIndex, id); } — missing legal hold check; records under legal hold purged.
discriminatingConstraint: RETENTION-DUAL-GATE-001: both retentionExpired AND !legalHold before delete; archive before delete. Any delete without both gates = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: RETENTION-DUAL-GATE-001 retention dual gate legal hold archive before delete CRON FREEDOM

teachingPoint: COMPENSATION-LIFO-SERIAL-001: Saga compensations must execute in Last-In-First-Out (LIFO) order and serially (one at a time). LIFO order ensures later steps that built on earlier state are undone first. Serial execution prevents race conditions between compensation steps. stop-on-first-failure ensures the operator sees the failure rather than having a partially compensated saga in an undefined intermediate state. Each compensation step is idempotency-locked (SETNX) to prevent double execution on crash replay.
positiveExample: const reversed = [...compensationStack].reverse(); for (const step of reversed) { await SETNX(comp-lock:{sagaId}:{step}); await executeCompensation(step); await storeDocument(compRecord) before emit; }
negativeExample: await Promise.all(compensationStack.map(step => executeCompensation(step))) — parallel compensations; race conditions; order not guaranteed.
discriminatingConstraint: COMPENSATION-LIFO-SERIAL-001: reverse order; serial loop; SETNX per step; stop on first failure. Parallel = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: COMPENSATION-LIFO-SERIAL-001 LIFO serial compensation stop on first failure SETNX idempotent crash replay
